title: gonystylus keithii
intro: Gonystylus keithii, also known as the Red Sandalwood or Balau, is a tropical tree species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. Its bark, heartwood, and leaves possess medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which provide its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gonystylus keithii can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to be used for its medicinal purposes.
